reasoning: USDA FSIS recalls involve an administrative process that includes effectiveness checks and verification of product disposal. While the LPK1 recall (issued Feb 24, 2025) involves a very small amount (303 lbs) of a highly perishable product (expired by late February), which simplifies the physical part of the recall, the transition of the official FSIS status from 'Active' to 'Closed' rarely occurs in under three months. Historical data suggests the median time to close an FSIS recall is often 6-12 months. Given that May 8, 2025, is only about 73 days after the recall was issued, and the resolution date of June 1 provides only about 97 days total, it is unlikely the administrative status change will be completed within this window. Any remaining stock would have been off shelves for months, but the bureaucratic closure is the bottleneck.
